events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: residential furnace & venting, condensate line without trap or sloped wrong. Work rough-complete. Need determine compliance per IRC M1801, NFPA 54. Condensate drainage: IRC M1801? Actually IRC Chapter 14 plumbing maybe. M1801 addresses condensate disposal? Let's recall IRC M1801: Condensate disposal. Requires condensate drains to be trapped and sloped min 1/8 per foot downward toward point of disposal, indirect waste receptor, etc. NFPA 54 maybe venting. The observed condition: condensate line without trap or sloped wrong. That fails code. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk? Condensate backup can cause water damage, furnace issues, maybe not critical but medium/high. Residential furnace condensate line missing trap or wrong slope can allow sewer gases? Actually trap prevents sewer gases and maintains drain. Wrong slope causes backup. Risk medium. Decision fail. Work stage rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 85. Value not relevant 0. Sound etc default 0. Findings: condensate drain line lacks required trap and/or is improperly sloped. Actions: install proper trap and ensure minimum slope per code/manufacturer. References: IRC M1801, NFPA 54? NFPA 54 maybe venting not condensate. But include both as requested. Need produce valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside JSON.
